evidence: src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx imports normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow from shared runtime error infrastructure (line 5) and routes provider-misuse guard failures through it (lines 45-55).

normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow in src/shared/lib/errorHandling/runtimeError.ts delegates to reportError/notifyError path before throwing (lines 14-21, 27-28), coupling foundational context guard failures to global error presentation/reporting.
This creates an unnecessary dependency edge from context boundary enforcement to app-wide runtime error plumbing, increasing blast radius for changes in shared error presenter behavior.

ShotActions exposes a large multi-bag contract (selector/status/quickCreate/actions) with many state setters and control callbacks (src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/ShotActions.tsx lines 18-66, 75-107).

useMediaGalleryHandlers similarly requires broad orchestration inputs and setter callbacks (src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts lines 14-27, 160-172), indicating responsibility split across call sites rather than a single owner boundary.

The same gallery interaction path still depends on choreography between setter-driven contracts and intent handlers, which keeps ownership diffuse and raises change coupling across gallery item, gallery handlers, and parent orchestration.

useSharedLightboxState still exposes/assembles a very broad contract (grouped input + large return surface) spanning variants, navigation, star, references, lineage, shots, source-generation, make-main, layout, and button groups in one abstraction (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts:122,137,618,648,727).

ImageLightbox constructs a large adapter object into that shared contract, then re-expands state into multiple presentation builders, indicating indirection cost remains high after split (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:200,298,591,733,794,852).

Video and image lightbox render models retain parallel orchestration surfaces with near-identical payload mapping, suggesting abstractions are split by file but not by behavior ownership (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:234,265; src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:733,794).

Task producers in the same subsystem use mixed payload contracts: some use family payload composition (joinClips/travel/individualTravelSegment) while others emit raw task params (magicEdit/videoEnhance), forcing readers to support multiple shapes (src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:390; src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:353; src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:739; src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts:208; src/shared/lib/tasks/videoEnhance.ts:244).

taskParamsUtils must keep broad multi-shape parsing heuristics and fallback precedence across orchestrator/task-view/raw paths, reflecting upstream contract drift coupling (src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts:36,110,279,292,322).

Travel/segment builders still carry legacy-compat cleanup paths in canonical builders (assert/strip legacy fields), increasing dependency on historical payload formats (src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:90,191; src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:489,493).

Image and video lightbox render models duplicate seam choreography for workflow bars/controls with nearly the same boundary fields, which increases drift risk when adding/removing workflow props (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:733,794; src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:234,265).

Both lightboxes independently map panel switching (edit vs info) with largely parallel inputs and task-id resolution logic instead of a shared seam adapter (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:437,717; src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:15,223).

Shot editor callback hook duplicates operation wrapper patterns for add-to-shot variants and create-shot, repeating error/result choreography instead of a shared operation adapter at the boundary (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:146,178,210).

useVideoLightboxRenderModel builds several large object literals (`lightboxStateValue`, `workflowBar`, `workflowControls`, `layoutProps`) without memoization, creating avoidable identity churn across provider/layout consumers (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:153,234,265,295).

The image counterpart already memoizes equivalent constructions, so local craft is inconsistent within sibling modules and increases accidental rerender pressure on the video path (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:591,630,733,794,852).

Inline edit state builder manually rehydrates very large view-model objects in one function, making local changes high-friction and error-prone due repetitive field wiring (src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:260,319,406).

No direct tests target `useGenerationTaskDetails` (no test references found for that symbol), despite async fallback mapping, error normalization, and conditional loading behavior in `src/shared/components/TaskDetails/hooks/useGenerationTaskDetails.ts`.

No direct tests target `timelineMutationService` functions (`persistSingleTimelineFrame`, `persistTimelineFrameBatchUpdates`, `syncTimelineGenerationFrames`) even though they encapsulate write-path timeout behavior and cache refetch choreography in `src/shared/hooks/timeline/timelineMutationService.ts`.
`useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` is only mocked in consuming tests (`useGalleryPageState.test.ts`) rather than contract-tested directly, leaving dialog-action integration unverified.

`src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts` uses `GenerationTaskCacheEntry` as a generic type argument at lines 56 and 109, but the repository exports `GenerationTaskMappingCacheEntry` (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts`), creating a split/invalid type surface.

`useGenerationTaskDetails` defines a local `GenerationTaskMapping` with only `taskId` and drops repository/cache status and query error semantics, while upstream contracts expose rich statuses (`ok`, `query_failed`, `scope_mismatch`, etc.).
Task-mapping API contract exists in multiple shapes across repository/cache/UI hook boundaries, with inconsistent fidelity and failure signaling.

Multiple assigned modules are comment-heavy pass-through shells with little behavior (`src/domains/generation/types/public.ts`, `src/shared/components/ui/theme/button.tsx`, `src/shared/lib/editVideoDefaults.ts`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/sections/generation/index.ts`).

Several files use long explanatory headers/doc blocks that restate implementation details rather than encoding non-obvious constraints (`src/shared/realtime/index.ts`, `src/shared/lib/galleryUtils.ts`, `src/tools/join-clips/utils/validation.ts`).
The pattern appears across unrelated areas (types, UI exports, utils), indicating broad AI-style verbosity/ceremony drift rather than isolated documentation needs.

Canonical-facing files still expose compatibility surfaces: `src/domains/generation/types/public.ts`, `src/shared/components/ui/theme/button.tsx`, and `src/shared/lib/editVideoDefaults.ts` are migration aliases still present in active source tree.

Legacy structures are still mixed into active UI/task contracts (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/uiTypes.ts` extends legacy fields; `src/shared/types/referenceImage.ts` exports `ReferenceImageWithLegacy`).
Deprecated shim remains in runtime path (`src/shared/compat/hooks/useExternalApiKeys.ts`) with import-time deprecation signaling, indicating migration not fully retired.

`src/shared/lib/editVideoDefaults.ts` is now a pure compatibility re-export, and a repo-wide search shows no remaining imports of `@/shared/lib/editVideoDefaults`, so legacy placement remains without active callers.

`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useExternalApiKeys.test.ts` still lives under the primary hooks test namespace even though it validates a compat shim at `../../compat/hooks/useExternalApiKeys`, leaving compat concerns in the main hooks package tree.

Holistic structure shows `src/shared/hooks/` (71 files) and `src/shared/lib/` (68 files), which increases cost of keeping legacy residues in top-level shared namespaces instead of isolating/removing them.
Most names are intent-revealing, but naming mixes appear around close concepts (`oauth intent`, `finalize`, `resolve on demand`) without a single vocabulary standard across auth/task-detail hooks.
Task modules mix singular/plural identity keys (`pair_shot_generation_id` vs `pair_shot_generation_ids`) which increases semantic ambiguity at call sites.
naming_quality.issues_preventing_higher_score: Terminology for equivalent concepts is still inconsistent across auth/task orchestration code.

evidence: Control flow is generally direct, but repeated fallback chains (especially task param extraction paths) create harder-to-verify precedence behavior.
Several hooks include multi-stage fallback behavior that is valid but non-trivial to reason about quickly (mapping cache -> fallback lookup -> task fetch).
logic_clarity.issues_preventing_higher_score: Fallback precedence logic is duplicated and long in a few places, reducing quick readability.

evidence: `useMobileInteractions` stores timer refs as `NodeJS.Timeout` in browser UI code, while sibling browser code (`ProductTour`) uses `ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>`.
This introduces environment-specific typing drift and can break stricter DOM-only TS setups.
`taskParamsUtils` declares a shared single-source parsing contract (`parseTaskParams`), but `useGenerationTaskDetails` reimplements ad-hoc JSON parsing instead of using the shared parser.

Task details paths now have divergent parsing behavior (`VideoTravelDetails` uses shared parsing utilities while `useGenerationTaskDetails` uses custom parsing), so equivalent inputs can produce different derived images.
Auth hooks rely on `runAuthSupabaseOperation` for normalized failures, but pre-wrapper `getSupabaseClient()` calls can throw raw errors before the operation wrapper is entered.
Error handling policy is therefore inconsistent between expected supabase/RPC failures and bootstrap-order failures.
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Early bootstrap failures bypass the standardized auth error wrapper path.

evidence: `getSupabaseClient()` hard-throws when `initializeSupabase()` has not run, creating strict boot-order coupling.
Multiple runtime hooks call `getSupabaseClient()` directly at action/query start, so initialization order failures propagate from shared global state rather than local explicit dependencies.
Timer typing convention is inconsistent across sibling UI hooks/components (`NodeJS.Timeout` vs `ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>`).
Some modules still mix quote/style and API shape conventions, though impact is minor compared with runtime contracts.
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: A few convention islands remain in shared UI/runtime code.

evidence: Reviewed in-scope auth hooks consistently route Supabase auth/RPC operations through dedicated auth operation wrappers.
No direct permission bypass pattern or inconsistent sibling auth checks were found in the assigned files.
authorization_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Authorization behavior is mostly consistent in this slice, but bootstrap-related failures can still bypass normal auth flow handling.

findings.summary: Project context guard errors are coupled to global runtime reporting/presentation
evidence: throwMissingProvider in ProjectContext calls normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow instead of throwing a local invariant error (ProjectContext.tsx lines 45-55).
normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow routes through reportError and conditional notifyError before throwing (runtimeError.ts lines 14-21, 27-28).
Provider misuse is a local invariant violation, but currently depends on cross-cutting runtime error infra.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a lightweight invariant error helper for provider guard failures (pure throw, no presenter/report side effects), use it in ProjectContext guard hooks, and reserve runtimeError presenter pipeline for user/runtime failures that need centralized reporting.

findings.summary: Gallery interaction ownership remains split across wide prop-bag orchestration contracts
evidence: ShotActions consumes four grouped contracts plus many setters/callbacks, then maps those into lower-level action controller inputs (ShotActions.tsx lines 18-66, 75-122).
useMediaGalleryHandlers accepts many cross-cutting setter/callback inputs for navigation, filter, task-details, and lightbox transitions (useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts lines 14-27, 160-216).

State handling remains distributed across separate hooks/components (including item-local state hook), so the interaction boundary is still coordination-heavy instead of centered in one controller owner.
findings.suggestion: Create a single gallery interaction controller boundary that owns shot-navigation/filter/task-detail/lightbox transitions and exposes narrow intent APIs plus read-only view models to item components; migrate ShotActions and handler hooks to consume that boundary instead of setter bags.

findings.summary: Shared lightbox abstraction remains a large multi-concern contract with high adapter overhead.
evidence: useSharedLightboxState input/return contracts aggregate many unrelated concerns in one interface tree (useSharedLightboxState.ts:122-246).
ImageLightbox builds a full adapter bag to feed shared state and then remaps outputs again into workflow/layout models (ImageLightbox.tsx:200-275, 733-880).
Video lightbox repeats near-equivalent model composition for variants/workflow/layout, indicating abstraction boundaries are still feature-crossing (useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:130-313).
findings.suggestion: Split shared lightbox APIs by intent (`useLightboxVariantsState`, `useLightboxWorkflowState`, `useLightboxPanelState`) and let Image/Video compose only required slices. Replace monolithic input bags with narrower contracts per slice to cut adapter mapping.

findings.summary: Image/video lightbox seams duplicate workflow and panel mapping logic with drift-prone parity code.
evidence: Both paths build similar `workflowBar` and `workflowControls` objects from `shotWorkflow/actions/sharedState` independently (ImageLightbox.tsx:733-850; useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:234-293).
Both paths independently decide panel content branches (edit/info) and resolve panel task ids (ImageLightbox.tsx:462-524, 717-730; useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:41-94, 219-231).
Layout consumes a single shared contract (`LightboxLayoutProps`), so duplicated upstream mapping is integration glue debt rather than intentional specialization (LightboxLayout.tsx:38-77, 167-205).
findings.suggestion: Create shared seam builders for `workflowBar`, `workflowControls`, and `panelTaskContext` used by both image and video controllers; keep media-specific deltas as explicit overrides only.

findings.summary: Video lightbox render hook recreates heavy state/layout objects each render, unlike image sibling.
evidence: `lightboxStateValue`, `workflowBar`, `workflowControls`, and `layoutProps` are plain object literals in useVideoLightboxRenderModel (useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:153-313).
Image lightbox memoizes equivalent structures (`buildVariantsModel`, `lightboxStateValue`, workflow models, layout model) to stabilize identities (ImageLightbox.tsx:591,630,733,794,852).
findings.suggestion: Memoize video render-model objects with `useMemo` (and shared builders where possible) to stabilize provider/layout prop identities and reduce avoidable rerenders.

findings.summary: Task creation modules depend on inconsistent payload contracts, forcing broad reader compatibility.
evidence: Family-contract builders and raw-task builders coexist in one subsystem (`composeTaskFamilyPayload` vs plain `composeTaskRequest`) (joinClips.ts:390-412; payloadBuilder.ts:353-374; individualTravelSegment.ts:739-767; magicEdit.ts:208-212; videoEnhance.ts:244-250).
taskParamsUtils includes broad multi-source parsing and precedence logic to reconcile these shapes (taskParamsUtils.ts:36-95, 110-166, 279-357).
Canonical builders still include legacy-shape guards/stripping, indicating active dependency on older schemas (payloadBuilder.ts:90-111; individualTravelSegment.ts:489-493).
findings.suggestion: Define one versioned task payload contract for all task families (including non-family tasks), migrate `magicEdit`/`videoEnhance` to it, and narrow `taskParamsUtils` to contract readers instead of heuristic field probing.

findings.summary: Critical task-details and timeline write orchestration paths lack direct tests.
evidence: `useGenerationTaskDetails` contains multi-branch async behavior (cache mapping + on-demand mutation fallback + parse/error normalization) with no dedicated test coverage.
`timelineMutationService` owns persistence calls and timeout-sensitive write behavior (`runTimelineWriteWithTimeout`) but has no dedicated tests asserting timeout/error/refetch behavior.
findings.suggestion: Add focused tests for these modules: (1) `useGenerationTaskDetails` cases for mapped task, unmapped task with successful fallback, query_failed fallback, and malformed `task.params`; (2) `timelineMutationService` cases for successful write, timeout propagation/ignoreTimeout behavior, and correct cache refetch calls.

findings.summary: Task-mapping cache code references a non-existent type name, diverging from repository API.
evidence: `generationTaskCache.ts` calls `setQueryData<GenerationTaskCacheEntry>` and `getQueryData<GenerationTaskCacheEntry>` (lines 56 and 109), but `GenerationTaskCacheEntry` is not defined/imported there.
The canonical exported type from repository is `GenerationTaskMappingCacheEntry`, indicating naming/contract drift at the boundary.
findings.suggestion: Unify on one exported cache-entry type (prefer `GenerationTaskMappingCacheEntry`) and replace `GenerationTaskCacheEntry` usages in `generationTaskCache.ts`; add a compile-time contract test (type-only) that cache and repository use the same mapping entry type.

findings.summary: Task-details seam discards mapping status and duplicates fallback choreography.
evidence: `useGenerationTaskDetails` maps cache data to `{ taskId }` only (lines 51-56), dropping `status`/`queryError` that upstream modules provide.
Because failure states are erased, the hook uses an additional mutation fallback path (`useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration`) without a single authoritative mapping-state model.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared `GenerationTaskResolution` contract (taskId + status + queryError + source) and pass it end-to-end from repository -> cache -> task-details hook; then gate fallback lookup explicitly by status (e.g., allow on `missing_generation`, skip on `query_failed` unless retry policy says otherwise).

findings.summary: Legacy/compat contracts remain exposed in active public modules, so migration debt is still embedded in day-to-day import paths.
evidence: `public.ts`, `ui/theme/button.tsx`, and `editVideoDefaults.ts` are explicit backwards-compat aliases.
`StructureVideoConfigWithMetadata` in `uiTypes.ts` composes `LegacyStructureVideoFields`, keeping legacy knobs in editor-facing state.
`ReferenceImageWithLegacy` is exported from shared types, allowing legacy field spread into new code paths.
`useExternalApiKeys.ts` is marked deprecated but still re-exported and executes deprecation enforcement at import time.
findings.suggestion: Quarantine migration-only types and shims into a dedicated compat boundary (single entry package), stop exporting legacy-augmented types from canonical modules, and execute a codemod + import-ban rollout so legacy aliases can be removed on a tracked deadline.

findings.summary: Documentation and wrapper ceremony is overproduced across simple files, adding noise and maintenance cost without behavioral value.
evidence: `realtime/index.ts` contains extensive architecture/usage prose around mostly export wiring.
`galleryUtils.ts` and `validation.ts` include large explanatory blocks and examples that restate local code behavior.
`generation/index.ts` and `ui/theme/button.tsx` are minimal re-export modules with extra narrative comments.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a terse-comment standard (`why`, not `what`), collapse trivial wrapper modules where possible, and move migration/usage prose to focused docs or ADRs instead of inline boilerplate.

findings.summary: Legacy compat artifacts still sit in primary shared package locations after migration.
evidence: `src/shared/lib/editVideoDefaults.ts` is only a compatibility re-export to `@/shared/settings/config/editVideoDefaults`.
No current `src/` imports reference `@/shared/lib/editVideoDefaults` (repo search returns none), so this compatibility alias is now an unneeded placement artifact.

`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useExternalApiKeys.test.ts` exercises `../../compat/hooks/useExternalApiKeys` but remains under `shared/hooks/__tests__`, keeping compat verification inside the primary hooks namespace.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Move compat-focused test to `src/shared/compat/hooks/__tests__/useExternalApiKeys.test.ts` so package intent matches ownership. (2) Remove `src/shared/lib/editVideoDefaults.ts` (or move it under `src/shared/compat/lib/` temporarily if external callers still need a shim). (3) Validate import/update state with `rg -n "@/shared/lib/editVideoDefaults|shared/lib/editVideoDefaults" src` and run targeted tests `pnpm vitest src/shared/compat/hooks/__tests__/useExternalApiKeys.test.ts src/shared/settings/config/editVideoDefaults.ts`.

findings.summary: A shared hook file also owns transport-level drag/drop helper exports, blurring module role.
evidence: In `useFileDragTracking.ts`, lines exporting `preventDefaultDragOver` and `createSingleFileDropHandler` are non-hook APIs forwarded from lower-level drag/drop utilities.
The same test suite validates hook state transitions and upload/drop helper behavior from one module, indicating mixed responsibilities in the public surface.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Keep `useFileDragTracking` in `src/shared/hooks/` as the hook-only API. (2) Move non-hook exports to a dedicated utility module such as `src/shared/lib/dnd/fileDropHandlers.ts` and stop exporting them from the hook file. (3) Update imports in callers to hook path for hook state and lib path for drop/upload helpers, then validate with `rg -n "createSingleFileDropHandler|preventDefaultDragOver" src/tools src/shared` and run `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useFileDragTracking.test.ts` plus affected page tests.

findings.summary: Supabase client access depends on hidden global init order and hard-fails when called early.
evidence: `getSupabaseClient()` throws when `hasInitializedRuntimeClient` is false.
`useDiscordSignIn` and `useAuthReferralFinalize` call `getSupabaseClient()` before entering their `runAuthSupabaseOperation` wrappers, so bootstrap-order faults bypass local error contracts.
`useTasks` query functions also depend on the same global initialization state.
findings.suggestion: Replace throw-on-uninitialized global access with an explicit lazy-safe accessor/result contract (e.g., `getSupabaseClientResult()`), and make callers handle that result inside their existing operation wrappers so initialization failures follow the same error pathway as RPC/auth failures.

findings.summary: Task param parsing is reimplemented in task-details hook despite a declared shared parser contract.
evidence: `taskParamsUtils` defines `parseTaskParams` as a shared parsing entry point.
`useGenerationTaskDetails` manually parses `task.params` with custom try/catch and bypasses `parseTaskParams`.
`VideoTravelDetails` uses shared parsing utilities, so equivalent payloads are processed through different parsing paths.
findings.suggestion: Centralize all task-param parsing in `parseTaskParams` and update `useGenerationTaskDetails` to consume it (then derive input images from the normalized record). Add a regression test proving malformed or non-object payloads resolve identically in both task-details paths.

findings.summary: Browser hook uses `NodeJS.Timeout` instead of environment-neutral timer typing.
evidence: `useMobileInteractions` declares `useRef<NodeJS.Timeout | null>` for a browser timeout.
`ProductTour` in the same UI layer uses `ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>`, which is the safer cross-env timer type.
findings.suggestion: Change `useMobileInteractions` timeout ref type to `ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null` and align timer typing convention across shared UI hooks to avoid Node-only type leakage in browser code.
